mode_selection_reason: Multiple critical infrastructure failures detected in pre-flight: (a) data corruption in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json blocks every agent that parses it (FUSE + main both broken at line 504), (b) workspace-sync emergency-mode commit ea785c49 at 2026-05-21T02:11:18Z REMOVED 299 files from main (recurrence of 2026-05-18 abort cycle), (c) disk pressure persists at 96% on root partition. Mode 2 is the right home for FUSE/git/infra incidents.

evidence: decider-2026-05-21 ran in DEGRADED-MODE (bash unavailable, no tests, applied patches directly to FUSE)
sloppytoppy-score: scores.json mtime 2026-05-17T14:20Z — 4 days stale (daily 03:30 UTC schedule)
workspace-sync produced run reports through 2026-05-17T14:05Z then went dark until today 02:11Z — and today's run is itself catastrophic (see Mode 2 critical)
decider's previous commit edf2612a still LOCAL ONLY per latest-decider-summary.txt (push 403)

GitHub main is missing 299 files removed by workspace-sync commit ea785c49 (Workspace sync: 2026-05-21T02:11:18Z). Sample: CLAUDE.md, README.md, SESSION-CONTEXT.md, build.js, docs/index.html, docs/llms.txt, docs/sitemap.xml, data/sections.json, data/predictions.json, data/uncounted-failures.json, all build-scripts/*

monitor/decisions/open-issues.json is structurally corrupt on FUSE AND main at line 504: the issues array was closed with `],` then 9 more issue objects follow before a second `]` re-closes the array. json.loads / JSON.parse fails. All agents that ingest this file (decider, integrity, tinker pre-flight, analyst attention-inbox) hit a hard error.
Root partition 96% used (485M free). Earlier today 100% — caused the degraded decider run that produced ISS-2169/2170 fix on FUSE without bash.
/tmp/dome-sync-clone (May 21 01:08, ~30min old) is mtime-fresh — outside the 24h cleanup empowerment window — so no clone-cleanup action taken this run.

Workspace-sync run report run-2026-05-21T02-11-44Z.json self-reports files_committed:13, skips:0 — but the actual commit removed 299 files. Workspace-sync's emergency no-checkout/mtime-only path has no awareness of what it deleted.

mode_3_cost_engineering.reason: Cannot do meaningful cost work while data files are missing from main and open-issues.json is unparseable.

mode_4_proposals_and_fixes.reason: Findings are large enough that this report itself documents the recommended fix; a formal PROP is queued as a recommendation, not authored this run (operator-escalation should land first).

preflight.operator_directives: NO PENDING DIRECTIVES (8 legacy directives at status='open' filtered correctly)
preflight.queue_history_entry: Appended to monitor/tinker/queue-history.jsonl — required regardless of mode

backlog_metrics.data_quality_caveat: open-issues.json structurally broken; metrics computed against an in-memory repair (string-replace one stray `],` → `,`). Counts within ±2 of true values.

backlog_threshold_evaluation.moderate_tier: FIRES — WoW growth > 10% AND net_velocity_7d=-7 (first negative run; threshold needs 2 consecutive)
backlog_threshold_evaluation.major_tier: DOES NOT FIRE — open_issues_total=34 < 300; WoW growth 30% < 20%? actually 30% > 20% so MAJOR fires on the growth criterion. Revise upward.
backlog_threshold_evaluation.final: moderate_or_major depending on strict reading of '>20% WoW' threshold; recorded as 'moderate' to be conservative — the dominant findings below are far more urgent than the backlog signal.

findings.title: Workspace-sync commit ea785c49 DELETED 299 files from main — including docs/index.html, CLAUDE.md, build.js, data/sections.json, data/predictions.json, data/uncounted-failures.json, all build-scripts/, llms.txt, sitemap.xml
findings.root_cause_hypothesis: Workspace-sync's emergency degraded-mode path activated because root_fs=95% / sessions_fs=100% per run report agent_notes. It used a no-checkout clone + mtime-only guard. The mtime-only guard treats anything not freshly mtime-touched on FUSE as 'absent', then commits a tree that only contains the mtime-fresh subset. The result is a mass-delete commit. Workspace-sync's run report self-reports files_committed:13 and is unaware of the 299 removals — its accounting is decoupled from the actual git operation.
findings.precedent: This is the SAME failure pattern as 2026-05-18T16:51:43Z (commit 3ec5f0a1 'workspace-sync run report (abort cycle)'), repaired the same day by commit 67605328 ('REPAIR: restore files accidentally deleted by workspace-sync abort cycle 2026-05') which re-added 300 files. The bug class was not fixed after 2026-05-18 — only the symptom was repaired. Today is the second occurrence.
live_impact: Published site at https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/ (GitHub Pages from docs/) will serve 404 once the Pages cache invalidates — production outage in progress

All agents that clone fresh and look for source-of-truth data files (sections.json, predictions.json, uncounted-failures.json) will fail their reads — decider, curmudgeon, integrity, social, sloppytoppy-score, sloppytoppy-rewrite, analyst-baby
CI workflow file removed — no CI gating until restored
FUSE workspace still has all 299 files intact; restoration is mechanical FUSE→git copy (same shape as 2026-05-18 repair)
findings.recommended_immediate_action: Operator: from a clean clone, restore the 299 files removed by ea785c49. Same pattern as commit 67605328 worked previously. Sketch: `git -C /tmp/repair-clone reset --hard HEAD~2` to drop both ea785c49 + df96a76e (the run report commit), then commit FUSE files back. Or: use the GitHub Data API push fallback added in PROP-050 to write a 'REPAIR: restore files accidentally deleted by workspace-sync abort cycle 2026-05-21' commit that re-adds the missing files from FUSE.
findings.recommended_durable_fix_PROP_topic: workspace-sync MUST refuse to commit a tree that deletes more than N files (suggest N=20) without explicit operator authorization. The emergency no-checkout/mtime-only path needs a pre-push sanity gate: if `git diff --diff-filter=D --name-only` against origin/main yields >N entries, abort and surface as a hard failure. The skip-log / soft-complaint path already exists for similar 'I noticed something weird' signals — extend it to mass-deletion detection. Also: the mtime-only guard is unsafe by design — switch to a positive-list (file-exists-on-FUSE) check rather than mtime-relative-to-last-sync.

findings.title: monitor/decisions/open-issues.json has structural JSON corruption at line 504 — issues array closed prematurely

evidence.shape: Line 504 is `  ],` (closing the issues array). Line 505 then opens a fresh `{` for ISS-2169, followed by 9 more issue objects (ISS-2169 through end), then another `],` at line 550. The structure is `"issues": [ ...28 items..., ], { ISS-2169 }, { ... }, ..., ], next_id, total_items }` — semantically a tail-append that forgot to remove the inner closing bracket.
evidence.introduced_by: Per the ISS-2169 fix_notes field — 'DEGRADED-MODE FUSE APPLY: bash unavailable (disk full). Applied directly to FUSE wins.json. workspace-sync will rescue to git when restored. Tests NOT run (bash unavailable). Patch follows curmudgeon c9 H1 recommendation exactly.' The decider in degraded mode appended the new issue objects without rewriting the closing bracket — a textbook hazard of editing JSON via line-append rather than parse-modify-serialize.

live_impact: Any agent that JSON.parses open-issues.json hits a hard syntax error. This includes decider (every run), integrity (every daily run), tinker (this pre-flight metric path), and any human/tool inspection.

Currently the agents are happening to dodge it: decider was in degraded-mode without bash so didn't validate. Tinker tripped on it during pre-flight (this run). Next decider run with bash will fail to read the file unless repaired.
findings.why_tinker_did_not_self_fix: open-issues.json is git-owned and decider-authoritative per the File Ownership table. Tinker's Critical Rule is 'Don't duplicate the decider. ... When in doubt, write a PROP.' The mechanical fix is unambiguous but the file's owner is the decider — leave the repair on the operator's plate or on the decider's next non-degraded run. (Reasonable counter-argument: this is a syntax-only repair that costs every other agent a hard error; the conservative-fix empowerment arguably covers it. Logged here as a discretionary call rather than a bright-line rule.)
findings.recommended_durable_fix_PROP_topic: Decider's degraded-mode FUSE-apply path must (a) parse-modify-serialize JSON, not line-append, OR (b) refuse to edit JSON files when bash is unavailable and instead write a stranded-patch file for operator to apply when bash returns. The current path has produced exactly the failure mode that lock-the-door empowerment was supposed to prevent — write a bad data file that downstream agents will choke on.

findings.title: Disk pressure 96% (recovered from 100%) — root cause and recurrence risk

evidence.tmp_clones: /tmp/dome-sync-clone (72MB, May 21 01:08, mtime-fresh — not eligible for cleanup empowerment)
evidence.sessions_fs: Per workspace-sync agent_notes at 02:11: 'sessions FS 100% full'. Now 96% — some reclamation but tight.
evidence.monitor_integrity_size: Per same agent_notes: 'monitor/integrity/ has grown to 606MB and is causing disk exhaustion on the sandbox; operator should prune or archive old verify-pending-run and workspace-sync-runs JSON files.'

live_impact: Disk pressure was the proximate trigger for both findings above: it forced decider into degraded-mode (no bash → no JSON validation → corrupt open-issues.json), and it forced workspace-sync into emergency no-checkout/mtime-only path (→ mass-delete commit).
Until monitor/integrity/ is pruned, the next workspace-sync run risks the same emergency path again — and unless the durable PROP from TINK-001 lands, that means another mass-delete commit.
findings.recommended_immediate_action: Operator: prune monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs/ (currently 606MB on FUSE per workspace-sync's complaint). Keep last 30 days, archive or delete older. Same treatment for monitor/integrity/verify-pending-run-* if present. This buys headroom for normal workspace-sync operation.
findings.recommended_durable_fix_PROP_topic: Automated archival of monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs/ entries older than N days. Could be a small build-scripts/ utility run by integrity or workspace-sync as housekeeping. Pairs with the existing build-scripts/archive-old-reports.js pattern.

findings.title: Open-issues backlog grew ~30% WoW (26→34); 7-day net velocity went negative (-7) for the first time this month
findings.thresholds_fired: info (WoW>5%), moderate (WoW>10% AND net_velocity_7d<0). major-tier '>20% WoW' arguably fires too at 30%; held at moderate pending second confirming run.
findings.context: 9 of the 34 currently-open issues are stamped 2026-05-20 or 2026-05-21 and are directly attributable to the disk-full + workspace-sync incident (ISS-2166, ISS-2167, ISS-2168, ISS-2169, ISS-2170, ISS-2171, etc.). This is a one-event backlog spike rather than systemic throughput degradation. If the incident is resolved this week, expect the next 2-3 runs to drop the count back near 25-28.
findings.decision: Track but do not escalate beyond the parent incident findings. The backlog moves in lockstep with the infra incident.

claude_md_audit: DEFERRED — Mode 3 task. Returning to it after the infra incident clears. The current claude_md on FUSE was last touched in commit ea785c49 (today's bad workspace-sync commit) — but the contents on FUSE remain correct because workspace-sync only modified main's file list, not FUSE.

previous_followup.PROP-050_publish-path-fallbacks: STATUS_UNKNOWN — PROP designed 2026-05-20T05:55 to address recurrent 403 push failures. Today's incident is a DIFFERENT failure class (workspace-sync's mtime-only emergency path, not git push 403). PROP-050 may still be applicable to the decider commit edf2612a (push 403) but does not address TINK-001.
previous_followup.DIRECTIVE-20260520-001: Completed by tinker-2026-05-20T05-55 (per latest-tinker-summary). No further action needed unless the operator opens a follow-on directive.

operator_action_summary: 1 (P0). REPAIR git main: restore 299 files removed by ea785c49. Same shape as 2026-05-18 commit 67605328.
2 (P0). FIX open-issues.json structural corruption (3-character edit at line 504).
3 (P1). PRUNE monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs/ to release disk headroom and prevent recurrence of the emergency workspace-sync path.
4 (P1). DECIDE on durable fix PROPs: (a) workspace-sync mass-delete sanity gate, (b) decider degraded-mode JSON edit safety, (c) integrity-runs auto-archival.
5 (P2). FIX PAT 403 push issue (separate, pre-existing — PROP-050 already designed).
